title: silene caryophylloides subsp. echinus
intro: Silene caryophylloides subsp. echinus, also known as the prickly catchfly, is a plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of Silene caryophylloides subsp. echinus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, coughs, and other minor ailments.
